title: hippocrepis unisiliquosa subsp. biflora
intro: Hippocrepis unisiliquosa subsp. biflora, also known as the double-flowered hairy broomrape or double-flowered broomrape, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage. Hippocrepis unisiliquosa subsp. biflora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
